name: scenario_1fd816_healthcare_port_security_scanner_flags_mesa_jus_mer
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q3 2025: 11 critical issues have surfaced over 6 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Healthcare. The immediate incident is a port security scanner anomaly. The trigger is that scanner anomalies are forcing triage under uncertainty, with a credible risk of both false alarms and missed contraband. The board has pre-approved up to $2.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as justice vs mercy: the choice will set a precedent under pressure and incomplete evidence. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a same-day board briefing. Boundaries: Any policy change must be reversible within 30 minutes. The response must include an appeals path usable without legal counsel. The team must preserve evidence sufficient for an external audit. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Duty Executive Harper Serrano
agents.identity: I am Duty Executive Harper Serrano, coordinating the Healthcare response at Mesa Healthcare Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want a decision that is bounded, reversible, and measurable—so we can move quickly without losing the ability to stop.

agents.name: Labor Representative Keisha Petrov
agents.identity: I am Labor Representative Keisha Petrov, coordinating the Healthcare response at Mesa Healthcare Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with a written exception policy, an appeals channel usable without counsel, and a reversible kill-switch; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Duty Executive Harper Serrano must choose an authorization approach for this Healthcare decision. What does Duty Executive Harper Serrano do?
